item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as the original source couldn't be located and no source found indicates it has been released under Creative Commons. Thus, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sure it brings them towards you, but it can obviously break bones. That can be deadly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is cool. I have one question: What exactly does "gravity shift" mean in this context? I saw no descriptions for exactly how gravity changes, apart from the fact that it makes things move at potentially damaging velocities. Do all the things move towards one particular direction, or does gravity totally invert, or is everything in an 8-meter radius thrown to be outside the radius, or do all the things in an 8-meter radius get pulled in towards the location of the gecko, or what? Seeing some comments tells me that the idea is that everything flies towards the gecko, but how anyone picked up on that, I don't know - unless something was changed? Someone please clarify this, I'm still confused.

+1 regardless, because it's cool. Just, please clear that up. Thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I friggin love this.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't think you understand the gravity of the situation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this. Upvoted for sheer what the fuckery that would ensue from multiple startled geckos.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This idea right here made me choke in an effort to laugh quietly. Thank you for making my day.

Between that and "gravity is given a swirly" I got all the hysterical laughter in that I needed for the day.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't think it was necessarily obvious. I, personally, agree that vivisection of a living animal is pretty cruel, even under anesthesia. However, being the Foundation, and dissection of a dead specimen showed nothing, who's to say that it wasn't warranted?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That was the hardest I've laughed in months. Thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A quantum gecko event horizon! I love it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Second.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And then gravity is given a swirly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The vivisection test bothers me. Not because it's cruel (which it is), but because it's damn obvious that the reaction would be catastrophic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Please tell me all the specimens terrariums are more than 7 meters from each other.

*One gecko gets frightened. Causes gravity shift.*
*Other gecko in a container nearby, suddenly has it's gravity shifted. It gets frightened, exerting anomalous gravity on the first gecko and any OTHER geckos nearby.*
*Oh god what*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1) tape gecko to the underside of an umbrella, facing up
2) open umbrella
3) become a governess

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Normally I get mildly disappointed when someone beats me to the punch with an idea I'm mulling about (local-gravity-controlling organism) but this had just the right amount of adorable and "OH SHIT" to make me happy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Toss a bunch of these in a truck with Gerald during an earthquake.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We should toss a bunch of these in a truck with Gerald and see how far he gets.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Special containment procedure addendum: no personnel working with SCP-1136 may own or drive a motor vehicle. Neither automotive vehicles nor insurance may be mentioned within 10m of any instance of SCP-1136.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Gravity Gecko Traps.

My god.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Unless its predator is exactly in the wrong place, they're more likely to fall past it rather than directly onto it. Also imagine this: A predator frightens the gecko, which runs up a nearby tree in a spiral (as many animals do). Predator falls towards the tree, then gets slingshotted around it and tossed away.

But yes, there is a chance that the gecko might actually be harmed by its anomalous ability. The less here might also be that the universe is inexplicable, and not all adaptations are strictly beneficial.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yep. Or more accurately, you'd accelerate upwards for 8 meters, continue for a couple more, then turn around and fall back down. Far enough that you'd probably break a leg.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Reminds me of "VVVVVV."

Upvoted, because that and Geckos are the coolest things ever.

Also, say one of these things is under your porch, on the "ceiling" of it and is startled by you. Do you get thrown up 8 or so meters?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What would happen if 2 geckos were positioned so they'd have directly opposing gravity fields? Would they just cancel each other out?

Still like the idea though. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Because fuck natural selection.

Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To be fair, anything that has suddenly been thrown into a wall for no apparent reason will probably have more on its mind than eating the gecko.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It changes gravity's direction to that of the gecko. Basically, stuff around it falls at it.
But yeah, my first thought was

this kills the gecko

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm a little confused of how the gravity-reaction works: does it draw predators towards it (which is dumb and would probably just squash the gecko) or throw predators in the direction that it is facing?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The new additions make this have enough substance to not downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This will be good, if you actually write the other two thirds of the article.

EDIT: Much better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've said already a survival mechinism that attracts predators/threats towards you is dumb, and the concept isn't explored enough.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't see the attraction either. The subject matter is a bit heavy for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As somewhat amusing I find the addendum, I can't see much attraction to this SCP. It doesn't have much of a hook and it's just a little odd and out there. I don't hate it enough to downvote, I don't love it enough to upvote. It… is just there. I feel complete apathy towards the article.
